nodes.label: Contract
nodes.title: NextEra_Corporate_Support_O_M_Agreement_2018_1
nodes.text:
1 
CORPORATE SUPPORT SERVICES, SHARED RESOURCES, 
AND OPERATIONS AND MAINTENANCE SERVICES AGREEMENT 
This Corporate Support Services, Shared Re sources, and Operations and Maintenance 
Services Agreement (the “ Agreement”), dated as of _____________ ___, 2018 (the “Effective 
Date”), is entered into by and between Next Era Energy Transmission MidAtlantic, LLC 
(“Company”) and NextEra Energy Resources, LLC, NextEra Energy Transmission, LLC, Florida 
Power & Light Company, and Lone Star Transmission, LLC (each, as such, a “ Provider” and 
collectively, hereinafter “Providers”).  Providers and Company are sometimes referred to herein 
as a “Party” and are collectively known as the “Parties.” 
RECITALS 
WHEREAS, Company is a Delaware limited liability company; 
WHEREAS, NextEra Energy Resources, LLC (“ NEER”) is a Delaware limited liability 
company; 
WHEREAS, NextEra Energy Transmission, LLC (“ NEET”) is a Delaware limited 
liability company; 
WHEREAS, Florida Power & Light Company (“FPL”) is a Florida corporation; 
WHEREAS, Lone Star Transmission, LLC (“Lone Star”) is a Delaware limited liability 
company; 
WHEREAS, Company and Providers are Affiliates that share a common parent; 
WHEREAS, Providers possess knowledge and skill in various aspects of transmission and 
business operations; 
WHEREAS, the provision of certain Corporate Support Services, Shared Resources, and 
Operations and Maintenance Services (collectively “ Services”) by Providers to Company will 
enable Company to obtain these Services effectively and efficiently; 
WHEREAS, Company desires to procure and obtain certain Services from Providers on a 
non-exclusive basis, and Providers are willing to provide such services and resources; 
WHEREAS, in keeping with the requirements of applicable laws, Company and Providers 
enter into this Agreement to memorialize the terms and conditions by which Providers will provide 
the Services to Company as set forth in this Agreement; and 
NOW THEREFORE, in consideration of the promises  and the covenants made herein, 
and for other good and valuable consideration, th e receipt and sufficiency of which is hereby 
acknowledged, Company and Providers hereby agree as follows: 
NEET MidAtlantic, LLC
Exhibit 3.8
2 
ARTI
CLE 1 
DEFINITIONS 
Section
1.1 Definition s.  Unless the context otherwise requires, the following terms 
shall have the following meanings, and such mean ings shall include the plural as well as the 
singular of each such term: 
 “ Affiliate” of a specified Person means any other Person that, directly or indirectly, 
controls, is controlled by, or is under common control or ownership with the Person.  “Control” of 
a Person (including, with correlative meanings , the terms “controlled by” or “under common 
control with”) means the possession, direct or indirect, of the power to direct or cause the direction 
of the management and policies of a Person, whether through the ownership of voting securities, 
by contract or otherwise. 
 “ Applicable Law ” means all applicable laws, statutes, treaties, rules, codes, 
ordinances, regulations, permits, o fficial guidelines, certificates, orders, interpretations, licenses 
and permits of any Governmental Authority, Go vernmental Approvals, environmental laws, and 
judgments, decrees, injunctions, writs, orders or like action of any court, arbitrator, or other judicial 
or quasi-judicial tribunal of competent jurisdiction and all requirements of law. 
 “ Business Day” means any day on which commerc ial banks are authorized to 
operate and not required to close in Juno Beach, Florida. 
 “ Code of Conduct” means the code of conduct of a Party, as may be amended or 
modified from time to time. 
 “ Corporate Support Services” means services such as joint corporate oversight, 
governance, support systems, and personnel shared by a utility, its parent holding company, or a 
separate affiliate as further described in Section 3.1. 
 “ Financing Agreements” means any credit agreement, reimbursement agreement, 
note purchase agreement, trust indenture, lease agreement, or other document (and any documents 
relating to or ancillary to the foregoing docum ents) under which Company or any Affiliate of 
Company obtains financing that is secured by all or substantially all of the assets of Company 
(including any credit enhancement for any bonds) for the acquisition, development, construction, 
modification, repair, or operation of a Company facility or any refinancing thereof or any equity 
take-out financing relating thereto. 
 “ Governmental Approvals ” means all applicable authorizations, consents, 
approvals, waivers, exceptions, variances, orde rs, franchises, permits, licenses, exemptions, 
publications, filings, and notices to and declarations of or with any Governmental Authority 
(including siting, occupancy, use, building, construction, and operating permits). 
 “ Governmental Authority” means any applicable federal, state, county, 
municipal, foreign, international, regional, or other governmental or regulatory authority, agency, 
NEET MidAtlantic, LLC
Exhibit 3.8
3 
board, body, commission, any arbiter pursuant to mandatory provisions of law, instrumentality or 
court, or any political subdivision of any of the foregoing. 
 “ Joinder Addendum” means a joinder addendum to this Agreement pursuant to 
which a Person agrees to be bound by the terms of this Agreement as a Provider hereunder. 
 “ Liability” or “Liabilities ” means any claims, actions, damages, expenses 
(including reasonable attorneys’ fees  and expenses incurred before trial or at trial or appellate 
levels), losses, payments, or other liabilities. 
 “ Operations and Maintenance Services ” means the services, competencies, 
processes, and tools provided under this Agreement to safely and reliably operate and maintain the 
Company and its properties and facilities as further described in Section 3.1. 
 “ Party” means a signatory to this Agreement, including any Person that signs on 
to this Agreement by executing a Joinder Addend um, and its wholly-owned subsidiaries, which 
subsidiaries may perform the obligations of a Party pursuant to this Agreement, provided, that with 
respect to any such subsidiary that has not ex ecuted a Joinder Addendum , the signatory to the 
Agreement is responsible for the actions of such subsidiary in its capacity as a Party to this 
Agreement. 
 “ Person” means an individual, a ssociation, institution, corporation, partnership, 
limited liability company, joint venture, trust or unincorporated organization, or a government or 
any agency or political subdivision thereof. 
 “ Secured Party ” means, collectively, each Person providing financing or 
refinancing under any Financing Agreement and any trustee or agent acting on any such Person’s 
behalf and their successors and assigns. 
 “ Shared Resources” means resources, support systems, or other resources that are 
not Corporate Support Services but are shared by a utility, its parent holding company, or a separate 
Affiliate as further described in Section
3.1. 
Section 1.2 References.  All references in this Agr eement to sections, paragraphs, 
clauses, schedules, appendices, and exhibits are to sections, paragraphs, clauses, schedules, 
appendices, and exhibits in and to this Agr eement unless otherwise indicated.  Any agreement 
defined or referred to herein shall include each amendment, modification, and supplement thereto 
and waiver, approval, and consent in respect ther eof as may become effective from time to time 
except where otherwise indicated, and includes references to all appendices, exhibits, schedules, 
and other attachments thereto and instruments, agreements, or other documents incorporated 
therein.  Any reference in this Agreement to any Person includes its permitted successors and 
assigns and, in the case of any Governmental Authority, any Person succeeding to its functions 
and capacities. 
Section
1.3 Rules of Construction .  The following rules of construction shall be 
followed when interpreting this Agreement:  (a) t itles and headings are in serted for convenience 
only and shall not be used for the purposes of construing or interpreting this Agreement; (b) words 
imparting the singular also incl ude the plural and vice versa; (c) words imparting one gender 
NEET MidAtlantic, LLC
Exhibit 3.8
4 
include the other gender; and (d) the words “include” and “including” are not words of limitation 
and shall be deemed to be followed by the words “without limitation”.
nodes.sourcePath: NextEra_Corporate_Support_O_M_Agreement_2018_1

nodes.label: Section
nodes.title: ARTICLE 2
nodes.text:
TERM 
Section
nodes.sourcePath: NextEra_Corporate_Support_O_M_Agreement_2018_1 > ARTICLE 2

nodes.label: Clause
nodes.title: 2.1 Term.  The term (“Term ”) of this Agreement shall commence on the
nodes.text:
Effective Date (or, with respect to any Party joining by Joinder Addendum, on the effective date 
of such Joinder Addendum) and sh all expire on the twentieth (20 th) anniversary of the Effective 
Date (the “Initial Expiration Date”).  This Agreement shall be subject to automatic extensions 
for additional five (5) year periods from the Initial Expiration Date or any subsequent expiration 
date (the last day of any extension period shall be referred to as the “Extended Expiration Date”), 
as the case may be, unless Company informs Providers in writing at least three (3) months prior to 
the Initial Expiration Date or a ny Extended Expiration Date that it does not intend to extend the 
Term of this Agreement.  Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Agreement is subject to earlier 
termination pursuant to this Article
2. 
Section
nodes.sourcePath: NextEra_Corporate_Support_O_M_Agreement_2018_1 > ARTICLE 2 > 2.1 Term.  The term (“Term ”) of this Agreement shall commence on the

nodes.label: Clause
nodes.title: 2.2 Termination.  This Agreement may be terminated without Cause by
nodes.text:
Company upon 180 days’ prior writ ten notice to Providers.  Pr oviders may only terminate upon 
sixty (60) days prior written noti ce, if, and only if:  (i) Compa ny is no longer an Affiliate of 
Providers, or (ii) by reason of an exercise of  any remedies by the Secured Parties under any 
Financing Agreements, Company ceases to own all of the assets and prope rties covered by this 
Agreement, or (iii) a Provider is required to terminate due to Applicable Law.   
Section
nodes.sourcePath: NextEra_Corporate_Support_O_M_Agreement_2018_1 > ARTICLE 2 > 2.2 Termination.  This Agreement may be terminated without Cause by

nodes.label: Clause
nodes.title: 2.3 Transition to New Provider .  Subject to any limitations resulting from a
nodes.text:
termination under Section 2.2, prior to or upon termination or e xpiration of this Agreement, 
Providers shall cooperate with Co mpany to effect an orderly transition of the Services being 
provided to Company to any ne w provider designated by Company.  Company shall reimburse 
Providers for costs incurred in c onnection with such termination af ter the effective date of such 
termination.
nodes.sourcePath: NextEra_Corporate_Support_O_M_Agreement_2018_1 > ARTICLE 2 > 2.3 Transition to New Provider .  Subject to any limitations resulting from a

nodes.label: Section
nodes.title: ARTICLE 3
nodes.text:
SERVICES 
Section
nodes.sourcePath: NextEra_Corporate_Support_O_M_Agreement_2018_1 > ARTICLE 3

nodes.label: Clause
nodes.title: 3.1 Corporate Support Services, Shared Resources, and Operations and
nodes.text:
Maintenance Services.   
(a) The Corporate Support Services to be performed under this Agreement shall be
as set forth and described in Exhi bit A to this Agreement, as such
Exhibit may be modified from 
time to time.  From and after the Effective Date, Providers shall provide and perform the Corporate 
Support Services consistent with the terms hereof. 
(b) The Shared Resources to be provid ed under this Agreement shall be as set
forth and described in
Exhibit B to this Agreement, as such Exhibit may be modified from time to 
NEET MidAtlantic, LLC
Exhibit 3.8
5 
time.  From and after the Effective Date, Providers shall provide the Shared Resources consistent 
with the terms hereof. 
(c) Operations and Maintenance Services to be provided under this Agreement
shall be as set forth and described in
Exhibit C to this Agreement, as such Exhibit may be modified 
from time to time.  From and after the Effective Date, Providers shall provide the Operations and 
Maintenance Services consistent with the terms hereof. 
(d) Company may obtain the Services to be performed and provided under this
Agreement from time to time on an as-needed basis.  No Party is under any obligation to procure 
or provide any minimum amount of Services under this Agreement. 
(e) The Parties shall perform and provide  the Services under this Agreement in
a professional and prudent manner in accordance with  standard industry practices for services of 
the type rendered under this Agreement; provided that Liability under this Agreement is limited as 
provided in Article 7 of this Agreement. 
(f) For the avoidance of doubt, the Part ies shall ensure that any Services
performed or provided under this Agreement are not prohibited by Applicable Law or 
Governmental Authority, except to the extent that any of the foregoing are permitted by exception 
or waiver granted by the applicable jurisdictional Governmental Authority. 
Section
nodes.sourcePath: NextEra_Corporate_Support_O_M_Agreement_2018_1 > ARTICLE 3 > 3.1 Corporate Support Services, Shared Resources, and Operations and

nodes.label: Clause
nodes.title: 3.2 Budget.   The Parties will cooperate reasonably to develop a budget for the
nodes.text:
Services for each calendar year (the “Budget”), provided that the Budget shall not be a limitation 
on the Services that the Company may request under this Agreement. 
Section
nodes.sourcePath: NextEra_Corporate_Support_O_M_Agreement_2018_1 > ARTICLE 3 > 3.2 Budget.   The Parties will cooperate reasonably to develop a budget for the

nodes.label: Clause
nodes.title: 3.3 Consultations.  The Parties shall consult regarding the pr ogress of the
nodes.text:
Services, any appropriate changes that should be made to such Services, the cost of such Services, 
where improvements can be made, where efficiencies can be gained, and other matters that either 
Company or Providers deem appropriate.  Compen sation for the Services to be paid under this 
Agreement is as set forth in Section 6.1 and Exhibits A1, B1, C1, and D.
nodes.sourcePath: NextEra_Corporate_Support_O_M_Agreement_2018_1 > ARTICLE 3 > 3.3 Consultations.  The Parties shall consult regarding the pr ogress of the

nodes.label: Section
nodes.title: ARTICLE 4
nodes.text:
CODE OF CONDUCT REQUIREMENTS; CONFIDENTIALITY 
AND COMPLIANCE WITH LAW 
Section
nodes.sourcePath: NextEra_Corporate_Support_O_M_Agreement_2018_1 > ARTICLE 4

nodes.label: Clause
nodes.title: 4.1 Code of Conduct.
nodes.text:
(a) Providers shall, at the Company’s expense, (i) comply with the Company’s
instructions relating to adherenc e to Company’s Code of Conduct,  if any, (a copy of which has 
been or will be delivered to Providers) as such code may be modified from time to time, (ii) work 
with the Company to comply with all additional Code of Conduct requirements imposed upon 
Company by a jurisdictional Governmental Authority or by Applicable Law (provided Company 
informs Providers of such additional requirements),  and (iii) at Company’s request, ensure that 
each employee of Providers that are providing Serv ices receives training in the Company’s Code 
NEET MidAtlantic, LLC
Exhibit 3.8
6 
of Conduct requirements, including training with respect to handli ng of confidential information 
and avoidance of inappropriate cross subsidization by Company of Providers or their Affiliates.   
(b) Company shall, at the Provider’s e xpense, (i) comply with each Provider’s
instructions relating to adherence to the Provider’s Code of Conduct (a copy of which has been or 
will be delivered to Company) as such code may be modified from time to time, (ii) work with the 
Provider to comply with all additional Code of Conduct requirements imposed upon Provider by 
a jurisdictional Governmental Authority or by ot her Applicable Law (provided Provider informs 
Company of such additional requirements), and (iii ) at the Provider’s request, ensure that each 
employee of Company receiving Services receives tr aining in the Providers’ applicable Code of 
Conduct requirements, including training with respect to handling of confidential information and 
avoidance of inappropriate cross-subsidization by Provider of Company or its Affiliates. 
Section
nodes.sourcePath: NextEra_Corporate_Support_O_M_Agreement_2018_1 > ARTICLE 4 > 4.1 Code of Conduct.

nodes.label: Clause
nodes.title: 4.2 Confidentiality.
nodes.text:
(a) Each Party shall keep any informa tion not intended for public disclosure
and considered to be confidentia l or proprietary by pe rsons privy to such information, including, 
but not limited to, information relating to the interconnection of customers to a utility's 
transmission or distribution systems, proprietary customer information, trade secrets, competitive 
information, and information about a utility's tr ansmission or distribution system, operations, or 
plans for expansion (“ Confidential Information ”) in strict confidence at all times, 
notwithstanding the completion or termination of  this Agreement; and no Party shall disclose 
another Party’s Confidential Information to others, nor shall a Party use the other Party’s 
Confidential Information for its own benefit (ex cept as necessary to pe rform its obligations 
pursuant to this Agreement) or the benefit of othe r parties, unless, in each instance, the receiving 
Party obtains the prior written consent of the disclosing Party. 
(b) Confidential Information shall not include:  (i) information which is or
becomes publicly available other than as a result of a violation of this Agreement; (ii) information 
which is or becomes available on a non-confidential basis from a source which is not known to the 
receiving Party to be prohibited from disclosing such information pursuant to a legal, contractual, 
or fiduciary obligation to the disclosing Party; (iii) information which the receiving Party can 
demonstrate was legally in its possession prior to  disclosure by the disclosing Party; or (iv) 
information which is developed by or for receiv ing Party independently of the disclosing Party’s 
Confidential Information.   
(c) Notwithstanding any of the terms of this Section 4.2, Confidential
Information may be disclosed by a receiving Party in response to a valid order or request of a 
Governmental Authority, or if it is otherwise requ ired to be disclosed by Applicable Law, or is 
necessary to establish the rights of either Party under this Agreement; provided, that, in the event 
the receiving Party believes it is so required to disclose the C onfidential Information, it shall 
promptly provide notice of such request or require ment so that the disclo sing Party may seek an 
appropriate protective order or take other action as it deems appropriate. 
(d) At any time upon the disclosing Part y’s written request, the receiving Party
shall return or destroy, at the receiving Party’s option, all written Confidential Information of the 
NEET MidAtlantic, LLC
Exhibit 3.8
7 
disclosing Party, and the receiving Party shall not retain any copies of such written Confidential 
Information; provided, however, that Confidentia l Information may be retained by the receiving 
Party to the extent that retention of such Confidential Information is necessary to comply with the 
receiving Party’s internal documen t retention policies aimed at le gal, corporate governance, or 
regulatory compliance, and any such retained Confidential Information shall remain subject to the 
disclosure and use restrictions set forth herein, for a period of 5 years from the termination of this 
Agreement.  The receiving Party shall, upon written  request of the disclosing Party, cause one of 
its duly authorized officers to certify in writing to the disclosing Party that the requirements of the 
preceding sentence have been satisfied in full.  Th e receiving Party shall not be deemed to have 
retained or failed to return or destroy any C onfidential Information if Confidential Information 
received or stored in digital format is deleted from local hard drives so long as no attempt is made 
to recover such Confidential Information from servers or back-up sources, provided that any such 
retained Confidential Information shall remain subject to the disclosure and use restrictions set 
forth herein, for a period of 5 years from the  termination of this Agreement.  
(e) Each Party acknowledges and agrees that any violation of this Section 4.2
may cause the other Party irreparable injury fo r which the disclosing Party would not have an 
adequate remedy at law, and the disclosing Party is entitled to seek immediate equitable relief 
including injunctive relief prohibiting any violat ion of this Section 4.2, in addition to any other 
rights or remedies available to such Party at law or in equity. 
Section
nodes.sourcePath: NextEra_Corporate_Support_O_M_Agreement_2018_1 > ARTICLE 4 > 4.2 Confidentiality.

nodes.label: Clause
nodes.title: 4.3  Compliance with Law.   In the performance of this Agreement, each Party
nodes.text:
shall comply with all Applicable Laws, Governmental Authority, and Governmental Approvals; 
provided, that interpretati on of Applicable Laws as they pert ain to the Code of Conduct or the 
operation of a Party shall be the ex clusive responsibility of such Party, and, to the extent a Party 
requests that Company or Providers, as applicable, comply with any such interpretation, such Party 
shall bear the expense of all costs associated with  such compliance.  Subj ect to this Section 4.3, 
each Party hereto shall be responsible to determin e for itself the laws, ordinances, regulations, or 
other legal requirements imposed upon its activities hereunder.
nodes.sourcePath: NextEra_Corporate_Support_O_M_Agreement_2018_1 > ARTICLE 4 > 4.3  Compliance with Law.   In the performance of this Agreement, each Party

nodes.label: Section
nodes.title: ARTICLE 5
nodes.text:
INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR RELATIONSHIP 
Section
nodes.sourcePath: NextEra_Corporate_Support_O_M_Agreement_2018_1 > ARTICLE 5

nodes.label: Clause
nodes.title: 5.1 Independent Contractor.   Providers shall undertake performance of the
nodes.text:
Services as independent contra ctors and not employees of Comp any.  The Parties shall work 
together in performing the Services under this Agreement.
nodes.sourcePath: NextEra_Corporate_Support_O_M_Agreement_2018_1 > ARTICLE 5 > 5.1 Independent Contractor.   Providers shall undertake performance of the

nodes.label: Section
nodes.title: ARTICLE 6
nodes.text:
COMPENSATION AND BILLING 
Section
nodes.sourcePath: NextEra_Corporate_Support_O_M_Agreement_2018_1 > ARTICLE 6

nodes.label: Clause
nodes.title: 6.1 Compensation.
nodes.text:
(a) The Services provided under this Agreement, and the compensation paid
therefor, shall not result in subsidization of th e Providers by the Company nor a subsidization of 
the Company by the Providers. 
NEET MidAtlantic, LLC
Exhibit 3.8
8 
(b) As compensation for performing any Services prior to the end of the Term
of this Agreem
ent, Company shall be charged the actual direct costs of the Service plus a 
reasonable allocation of indirect and overhead costs in accordance with Exhibits A1, B1, C1, and 
D and as required by Applicable Law or Governme ntal Authority.  Nothing in this Section 6.1 is 
intended to prohibit the Parties from agreeing to per unit charge s for certain Services, provided 
that the per unit prices b illed shall be trued-up to actual costs on at least an annual basis, so that, 
on an annual basis, the Party receiving the Services shall pay no more and no less than the actual 
direct and indirect costs and expenses of providing such Services.   
(c) Notwithstanding anything to the co ntrary herein, the amounts charged for
the Services provided under this Agreement shall be no higher than the amounts charged by a 
Provider to its other Affiliates or to a non-affiliated Person (within the same market area or having 
the same market conditions) for the same service or class of services. 
Section
nodes.sourcePath: NextEra_Corporate_Support_O_M_Agreement_2018_1 > ARTICLE 6 > 6.1 Compensation.

nodes.label: Clause
nodes.title: 6.2 Billing.
nodes.text:
(a) The Parties will maintain accurate r ecords of all direct and indirect costs
and expenses incurred in the performance of the Services under this Agreement.  A Provider 
providing any such Services shall render a monthly invoice or accounting system generated report 
summarizing the direct and allocated costs and expenses for that month.  Each monthly invoice or 
accounting system generated report will be reviewed before approval of the charges, including any 
documentation necessary to support the charges fo r the Services provided, to ensure the charges 
are properly billed.  
(b) In cases in which a Provider renders no Services itself but merely pays third
parties for products or services provided under this Agreement (as a matter of convenience to the 
receiving Party), the Provider shall be reimbur sed for any and all reasonable, ordinary, and 
necessary out-of-pocket expenses actually incurred with respect to such products or services and 
shall provide supporting documentation of such payments as requested by Company.
nodes.sourcePath: NextEra_Corporate_Support_O_M_Agreement_2018_1 > ARTICLE 6 > 6.2 Billing.

nodes.label: Section
nodes.title: ARTICLE 7
nodes.text:
EXONERATION; LIMITATION OF LIABILITY; INDEMNIFICATION 
Section
nodes.sourcePath: NextEra_Corporate_Support_O_M_Agreement_2018_1 > ARTICLE 7

nodes.label: Clause
nodes.title: 7.1 Limitation of Liability.  Notwithstanding any provi sion herein to the
nodes.text:
contrary, none of Company or Providers nor any of their respective officers, members, 
shareholders, partners, principals, Affiliates, agents subcontractors, vendors, or employees shall 
be liable hereunder for punitive, indirect, consequential, or exemplary losses or damages of any 
nature, including damages for lost profits or revenues or the loss  or use of such profits or 
anticipated revenues, cost of capital, loss of goodwill, increased operating expenses of plant or 
equipment, increased costs of purchasing or providing equipment, materials, labor, services, costs 
of replacement power or capital, pe nalties, inventory or use charges, damages to reputation or 
damages for lost opportunities, or any other special or incidental damages, regardless of whether 
said claim is based upon contract, warranty, tort (including negligence and strict liability), or other 
theory of law. 
NEET MidAtlantic, LLC
Exhibit 3.8
9 
Section
nodes.sourcePath: NextEra_Corporate_Support_O_M_Agreement_2018_1 > ARTICLE 7 > 7.1 Limitation of Liability.  Notwithstanding any provi sion herein to the

nodes.label: Clause
nodes.title: 7.2 Indemnification.
nodes.text:
(a) Company shall, to the fullest extent permitted by law, defend, indemnify,
and hold harmless Provider and its Affiliates from and against any and all Liabilities claimed or 
asserted against Provider or its Affiliates by a th ird party arising as a result of or in any way 
connected with the Company’s failure to perform under this Agreement, except to the extent such 
failure was the result of fra ud, willful misconduct, violation of Applicable Law, or gross 
negligence on the part of Provider. 
(b) Provider shall, to the fullest extent permitted by Applicable Law, defend,
indemnify, and hold harmless Company from and against any and all Liabilities claimed or 
asserted against Company by a third party arising as a result of Provider’s (or any of its Affiliates’) 
failure to perform under this Agreement, except to the extent such Liabilities are caused by fraud, 
willful misconduct, violation of Applicable Law, or gross negligence by Company. 
(c) If any Party hereto (each, an “ Indemnified Party”) shall receive notice or
have knowledge of any claim that may result in a claim for indemnification by such Indemnified 
Party against a Party pursuant to th is Section  7.2, such Indemnifie d Party shall, as promptly as 
possible, give the indemnifying Party notice of such claim, in cluding a reasonably detailed 
description of the facts and circ umstances relating to such claim, and a co mplete copy of all 
notices, pleadings, and other papers  related thereto, a nd in reasonable detail the basis for its 
potential claim for indemnifi cation with respect thereto; provided that failure promptly to give 
such notice or to provide such information a nd documents shall relieve the indemnifying Party 
from the obligation hereunder to respond to or to defend the Indemnified Party failing to give such 
notice against such claim only to the extent such failure prejudiced the interests of the 
indemnifying Party with respect to such claim.  The Party against whom indemnification is claimed 
shall, upon its acknowledgement in writing of its obligation to indemnify the Indemnified Party 
seeking indemnification, be entitled to assume th e defense or to represen t the interests of the 
Indemnified Party seeking indemnification in respect of such claim, which shall include the right 
to select and direct legal counsel  and other consultants, appear in  proceedings on behalf of such 
Indemnified Party, and to propose, accept, or reject offers of settlement, all at its sole cost; 
provided, however , that without the Indemn ified Party’s consent, which consent may not be 
unreasonably withheld, the indemnifying Party ma y not consent to entry of a judgment or 
settlement if such judgment or settlement provides for injunctive or other nonmonetary relief 
affecting the Indemnified Party.
nodes.sourcePath: NextEra_Corporate_Support_O_M_Agreement_2018_1 > ARTICLE 7 > 7.2 Indemnification.

nodes.label: Section
nodes.title: ARTICLE 8
nodes.text:
BOOKS AND RECORDS; AUDITS 
Section
nodes.sourcePath: NextEra_Corporate_Support_O_M_Agreement_2018_1 > ARTICLE 8

nodes.label: Clause
nodes.title: 8.1 Books and Records.   The Parties shall keep accurate records and books of
nodes.text:
account in respect of the Services provided pursuan t to this Agreement.  The Parties shall make 
those records and books of account available for inspection and audit in accordance with the terms 
of this Agreement. 
Section
nodes.sourcePath: NextEra_Corporate_Support_O_M_Agreement_2018_1 > ARTICLE 8 > 8.1 Books and Records.   The Parties shall keep accurate records and books of

nodes.label: Clause
nodes.title: 8.2 Audits.
nodes.text:
NEET MidAtlantic, LLC
Exhibit 3.8
10
(a) The Parities shall have the right, at their expense, to engage independent
auditors to perform
 a periodic audit of the records and books for the Services provided pursuant to 
this Agreement to verify compliance with Applicable Law.  If any audit or review of a Party’s 
books and records discloses that the allocated charge billed for the Services is not consistent with 
this Agreement such that an overpayment or an underpayment has been made, the amount of such 
overpayment or underpayment shall promptly be paid to the Party to which it is owed by the other 
Party.  Any audit to be conducted shall be performed timely such that the period being reviewed 
shall be no more than three years prior to the date of the initiation of the audit.
nodes.sourcePath: NextEra_Corporate_Support_O_M_Agreement_2018_1 > ARTICLE 8 > 8.2 Audits.

nodes.label: Section
nodes.title: ARTICLE 9
nodes.text:
NOTICES 
Section
nodes.sourcePath: NextEra_Corporate_Support_O_M_Agreement_2018_1 > ARTICLE 9

nodes.label: Clause
nodes.title: 9.1 Notices.  All notices required or permitted  under this Agreement shall be in
nodes.text:
writing and shall be hand-delivered or sent by certified or registered mail (return receipt requested), 
or commercial delivery subject to written record of receipt, to Company or Provider, as the case 
may be, at their respective addresses set forth below, or to such other addresses as may be 
designated by notice given as here in required.  All notices shall be  effective upon first receipt as 
evidenced by written record of delivery or confirmation of transmission. 
Company: NextEra Energy Transmission MidAtlantic, LLC 
700 Universe Boulevard

nodes.text:
MISCELLANEOUS 
Section
nodes.sourcePath: NextEra_Corporate_Support_O_M_Agreement_2018_1 > ARTICLE 10

nodes.label: Clause
nodes.title: 10.1 Counterparts.   This Agreement may be executed in two or more
nodes.text:
counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original, but all of which together shall constitute 
one and the same instrument. 
Section
nodes.sourcePath: NextEra_Corporate_Support_O_M_Agreement_2018_1 > ARTICLE 10 > 10.1 Counterparts.   This Agreement may be executed in two or more

nodes.label: Clause
nodes.title: 10.2 Assignment.   The rights under this Agreement shall not be assignable or
nodes.text:
transferrable nor the duties delegable by any Part y without prior written consent of the other 
Parties, which consent shall not be unreasonabl y withheld or delayed; and nothing in this 
Agreement, express or implied, is intended to c onfer upon any Person or entity, other than the 
Parties hereto and their permitted successors-in-interest and permitted assignees, any rights or 
remedies under or by reason of this Agreement unle ss so stated to the contrary.  Notwithstanding 
the foregoing: 
NEET MidAtlantic, LLC
Exhibit 3.8
12 
 (a) Company or Provider may grant to a Secured Party a security interest in its 
rights under this Agreement, provided that neither the grant of any such interest, nor the foreclosure 
of any such interest, shall in a ny way release, reduce, or diminish the obligations of such Party 
hereunder.  In the event of such security assignment, the assigning Party will execute for the benefit 
of such Secured Party a consent and agreement. 
 (b) This Agreement may be assigned by a Party, with ninety (90) days’ prior 
written notice to the other Parties:  (i) to the su ccessor of the assigning Party, if such successor is 
an Affiliate of such Party; or (ii) to an Affiliate of the assigning Party acquiring all or a controlling 
interest in the business assets of such Party.  
Section
nodes.sourcePath: NextEra_Corporate_Support_O_M_Agreement_2018_1 > ARTICLE 10 > 10.2 Assignment.   The rights under this Agreement shall not be assignable or

nodes.label: Clause
nodes.title: 10.3 Force Majeure.   No Party hereto shall be in breach or in vi olation of this
nodes.text:
Agreement if such Party is prevented from perf orming any of its obligations hereunder for any 
reason beyond its reasonable control, including acts of God, fire, flood, earthquake, explosion, riot, 
war, sabotage, or governmental acts and decrees that in fact prevent performance hereunder. 
Section
nodes.sourcePath: NextEra_Corporate_Support_O_M_Agreement_2018_1 > ARTICLE 10 > 10.3 Force Majeure.   No Party hereto shall be in breach or in vi olation of this

nodes.label: Clause
nodes.title: 10.4 Contracting .  The Parties may contract on their own behalf with unaffiliated
nodes.text:
third parties for auditing services, legal advice, and other third party professional services for the 
performance of duties to be performed under this Agreement. 
Section
nodes.sourcePath: NextEra_Corporate_Support_O_M_Agreement_2018_1 > ARTICLE 10 > 10.4 Contracting .  The Parties may contract on their own behalf with unaffiliated

nodes.label: Clause
nodes.title: 10.5 Subsidiary Employees.  The Parties acknowledge th at all or a portion of the
nodes.text:
obligations hereunder may be performed with the assistance of a Party’s wholly-owned 
subsidiaries and employees thereof. 
Section
10.6 Waiver.   The waiver of any breach of a ny terms or conditions hereof shall 
not be deemed a waiver of any other subsequent breach, whether of like or different nature. 
Section
nodes.sourcePath: NextEra_Corporate_Support_O_M_Agreement_2018_1 > ARTICLE 10 > 10.5 Subsidiary Employees.  The Parties acknowledge th at all or a portion of the

nodes.label: Clause
nodes.title: 10.7 Severability.   If any term or provision of this Agreement or the performance
nodes.text:
thereof shall to any extent be invalid or unenfor ceable, such invalidity or unenforceability shall 
not affect or render invalid or unenforceable an y other provision of this Agreement, and this 
Agreement shall be valid and enforced to the fullest extent permitted by Applicable Law. 
Section

nodes.text:
valid unless in writing, executed by the Parties hereto.

nodes.label: Clause
nodes.title: 10.9 Governing Law.   This Agreement shall be governed by and construed under
nodes.text:
the laws of the State of Florida without regard to its conflict of laws principles.  TO THE EXTENT 
PERMITTED BY LAW, EACH OF THE PARTIES HERETO HEREBY KNOWINGLY, 
VOLUNTARILY AND INTENTIONALLY WAIVES  THE RIGHT EITHER OF THEM MAY 
HAVE TO A TRIAL BY JURY IN RESPECT OF ANY LITIGATION BASED HEREON, OR 
ARISING OUT OF, UNDER OR IN CONNECTION WITH THIS AGREEMENT.   
Section
nodes.sourcePath: NextEra_Corporate_Support_O_M_Agreement_2018_1 > ARTICLE 10 > 10.9 Governing Law.   This Agreement shall be governed by and construed under

nodes.label: Clause
nodes.title: 10.10 Entire Agreement.   This Agreement and all exhibits and schedules hereto
nodes.text:
constitutes the entire Agreement between the Pa rties pertaining to the subject matter contained 
herein and supersedes all prior agreements, arrangements, understandings, representations, 
warranties, commitments, and communications (o ral or written) related to the subject matter 
hereof.  The Parties make no representation or warranties except as set forth in this Agreement, 
the exhibits, and schedules.  By execution of this Agreement, each of the Parties represents and 
NEET MidAtlantic, LLC
Exhibit 3.8
13
warrants that it has relied on no oral or written statements, promises, inducements, representations, 
or warranties to enter into this Agreement excep t for those expressly set forth herein, and that 
inclusion of this provision evid ences the intent of the Parties that no parole evidence shall be 
admissible to alter or vary the terms of this Agreement. 
[Signatures follow on separate page(s).] 
NEET MidAtlantic, LLC
Exhibit 3.8
14

nodes.label: Exhibit
nodes.title: EXHIBIT A
nodes.text:
SCOPE OF CORPORATE SUPPORT SERVICES 
Subject to reasonable lim
its on the timing and scheduling of resources and as agreed to by 
Providers and Company management for the performance of day to day operational needs, certain 
Corporate Support Services shall be provided as  may from time to time be requested.  A non-
exclusive list of Corporate Suppor t Services that may be provided includes, but is not limited to 
the items on the list below.  The Provider or Prov iders that will provide each service, along with 
the billing methodology that will be used by the Pr ovider to charge for the service, is shown on
Exhibit A1. 
• Administrative Services
o Administrative services include all ot her forms of support provided to the
Company, not specifically listed below, in cluding, without limitation, insurance,
risk management, building and facilities management, telecommunications, and
public relations.
• Accounting and Finance
o Accounting and Finance services include maintaining the books and records which
document the financial transactions of th e Company.  Specific  services include
Accounts Payable, Accounts Receivable, Billing, Tax, Financial Reporting, and
updating the accounting system.  Accounting and Finance services include
overseeing Sarbanes-Oxley compliance in general; maintaining the general ledger
to produce reports based on the FERC Uniform System of Accounts and record and
track all construction costs, in accorda nce with the approved work breakdown
structure and regulatory requirements.  Accounting and Finance services also
include responsible for maintaining adequate controls such that the Company’s
books are complete and accurately reflect the activities of the Company.
• Business Management Services
o Executive oversight and strategic direction for business planning, development, and
operations.  Development and implementation of strategies, processes, and policies
for financial planning and forecasting, affiliate transactions, corporate compliance,
and business activities.
• Corporate Real Estate
o Corporate Real Estate services include identifying the location for office space and
material yards; assisting with the leasing of such space; planning office space and
furniture use allocation; pr eparation of requests for pr oposal; and assistance with
construction build-out of Company’s premises.
NEET MidAtlantic, LLC
Exhibit 3.8
ii
Exhibit A
• Environmental
o Environmental Services include provi ding direction and management for
environmental analysis and studies, reviews and preparation of permit applications,
consulting with agencies, negotiating permit conditions, and supporting public
outreach activities.  Environmental Servi ces include functioning as the corporate
subject matter expert for environmental issues and the environmental aspects of the
project development process.  As su ch, Environmental Services include
maintaining environmental assurance to  corporate governance policies through
regular review of the Comp any’s activities, practices, pr ocesses, and indicators.
Environmental Services also include providing other en vironmental support
functions, such as, on-site reviews and inspections, as needed.
• Human Resources
o Human Resources (HR) services incl ude compensation, recruiting, hiring and
terminating employees, benefits, employee relations, and development functions.
These will include services such as esta blishing and maintain ing personnel data,
and developing career paths.  HR services include maintaining compliance with
Sarbanes-Oxley Section
nodes.sourcePath: NextEra_Corporate_Support_O_M_Agreement_2018_1 > EXHIBIT A

nodes.label: Clause
nodes.title: 404.  HR servic es also include performing any other
nodes.text:
employee related function and human resources support as needed.
• Information Management (IM)
o IM services include implementing and ma intaining all comput er applications
required by the Company to conduct its business.  Se rvices include hardware
installation and support, software applic ation upgrades, cyber-security, database
structure and controls, desktop support and other computer services as needed.
• Legal
o Legal services include providing legal ad vice and contract support for regulatory,
financial, corporate oversight, labor and employment, real estate transactions, and
disputes and damages arising during the course of the Company’s business.
• Procurement/Integrated Supply Chain (ISC)
o Procurement/ISC services include performing all necessary services to support the
Company in procuring needed materials and services, including qualifying vendors
and maintaining a vendor database; soliciting proposals for goods and services,
including preparing requests for proposal s, distributing the same to vendors, and
assisting in proposal evaluations; issuing purchase orders and contracts;
maintaining standard terms and cond itions; supporting supplier diversity by
identifying opportunities, a nd other support functions as  needed related to the
procurement of goods and services.
NEET MidAtlantic, LLC
Exhibit 3.8
iii
Exhibit A
• Regulatory Affairs
o Regulatory Affairs services include coordinating dockets for regulatory rulemaking
policy, rate proceedings, and other technical filings before Governmental
Authorities.  This includes coordinati on of case planning activities, strategy
development, witness selection and preparation, filing logistics, discovery protocol,
hearing logistics, and other applicable services.
• Treasury
o Treasury services include maintaini ng bank accounts, managing the cash, and
making investments of funds and disbursements as directed.  In addition, Treasury
services include securing financing,  ongoing compliance and monitoring with
regards to existing financings, and any other treasury functions required to manage
finances.
NEET MidAtlantic, LLC
Exhibit 3.8
i
Exhibit B
nodes.sourcePath: NextEra_Corporate_Support_O_M_Agreement_2018_1 > EXHIBIT A > 404.  HR servic es also include performing any other

nodes.text:
SCOPE OF SHARED RESOURCES 
Subject to reasonable lim
its on the timing and scheduling of resources and as agreed to by 
Providers and Company management for the performance of day to day operational needs, certain 
Shared Resources shall be provided as may from time to time be requested.  A non-exclusive list 
of Shared Resources that may be provided includes, but is not limited to the items on the list below. 
The Provider or Providers that will provide each service, along with the billing methodology that 
will be used by the Provider to charge for the service, is shown on
Exhibit B1.
nodes.sourcePath: NextEra_Corporate_Support_O_M_Agreement_2018_1 > EXHIBIT B

nodes.label: Clause
nodes.title: 1. Energy Management Systems (“EMS”) Related Shared Resources:
nodes.text:
a. Hardware and Software licenses and ongoing maintenance for EMS-related
infrastructure and applications shared by Company and Provider as well as
support applications and systems used to monitor, troubleshoot, and update
Company and Provider cyber infrastructure.
b. Software applications and systems utilized for asset tracking and change
management of EMS-related infrastructure.
c. Software and systems utilized in the authentication and provisioning of access to
users of EMS infrastructure.
d. Holistic management and monitoring of all cyber systems related to NERC
Critical Infrastructure Protection compliance.

nodes.text:
other aids for tracking operational performance of Company.

NEET MidAtlantic, LLC

nodes.text:
SCOPE OF OPERATI
ONS AND MAINTENANCE SERVICES 
Subject to reasonable limits on the timing and scheduling of resources and as agreed to by 
Providers and Company management for the performance of day to day operational needs, certain 
Operations and Maintenance Services shall be provided as may from time to time be requested.  A 
non-exclusive list of Operations and Maintenance Se rvices that may be provided includes, but is 
not limited to the items on the list below.  The Provider or Providers that will provide each service, 
along with the billing methodology th at will be used by the Provider to charge for the service, is 
shown on
Exhibit C1. 
• System Operations
o Planning, coordination, and execution of real-time operati ons of transmission and
substation systems
• Operational Compliance
o Compliance program management
o Sustainability of daily operations compliance
o Coordination of NERC and regional information requests
o Coordination of audit documentation
• Transmission Business Services
o Accounting
o Financial services and planning
• Safety, Environmental, and Training support
• Field Support Services
o Practices and procedures
o Technical training
o Peer reviews
o Relay setting development
o Forensic analysis
o Equipment specifications
o Vegetation management
• Engineering and Construction
o Cost and scheduling activities
o Engineering scope of work documents for transmission and substation activity
o Development of specifications, bid docum
 ents, and related materials
o Project management for construction projects
• Procurement/Integrated Supply Chain (ISC)
NEET MidAtlantic, LLC
Exhibit 3.8
ii
Exhibit C
• Emergency Preparedness and Response
o Emergency management plans
o Business continuity
o Emergency support personnel
• Transmission Services and Planning
• Grid Technology
o Predictive equipment, analytics and diagnostics
NEET MidAtlantic, LLC
Exhibit 3.8
